title: thymus serpyllum subsp. britannicus
intro: Thymus serpyllum subsp. britannicus, commonly known as wild thyme, is a subspecies of the thyme plant native to the United Kingdom, known for its small, pink or purple flowers and a fragrance similar to lemon. The medicinal properties of wild thyme include its antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and congestion. The health benefits of wild thyme include its ability to soothe digestive issues, boost the immune system, and act as an antiseptic to prevent infections. Wild thyme can be prepared as a tea, infused oil, or added to food as a flavoring agent, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine to promote overall well-being.
